title: Live-cell microscopy for mitochondrial membrane potential measurements
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"ea5g0","text":"Protocol for live-cell microscopy for mitochondrial membrane potential measurements","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Seed HeLa TMEM192-3xHA cells of choice in a µ-Slide 8-well, glass bottom plates (ibidi, #80807) and further cultured in the vessel until reaching appropriate confluency for microscopy.

Incubate cells with TMRE (1:5000) and MitoTrackerDeepRed (1:10000) for one hour at 37°C, wash twice with PBS and replace growth media before imaging. If possible image in phenol-red free FluoroBrite DMEM media.
Transport cells to microscope and keep at 37°C until instrument is ready for experiment.

Mount glass bottom plate on a Yokogawa CSU-X1 spinning disk confocal on a Nikon Eclipse Ti2-E motorized microscope. Ensure that the system is equipped with a Tokai Hit stage top incubator and imaging was performed at 37°C, 5% CO2 and 95% humidity.
Take image stacks using a Nikon Plan Apo 60×/1.40 N.A immersion oil objective lens.
Image at 5 % laser power and 100 ms (568 nm) or 50 ms (640 nm) exposure time was used to image 6 µm z-stacks of cells.
Perform image quantification was in your tool of choice. Here we will use ImageJ/FiJi and custom-written batch-macros.
Create a mitochondrial mask to only measure form the mitochondrial area. Invert mask. Subtract this mask from both separate channels to have a clean mitochondrial signal for subsequent evaluation.
Divide the TMRE and the mtDR channels to create the ratio of the two signals. Measure the resulting stack.
Save the intensity measurements per stack as .csv file.
Plot results in your tool of choice for graphing and statistical analysis.
creator: Felix Kraus
